teamObjective.explanation: The partner-near-finish scenario dominates. Leading the lowest card (3♦) best supports the team objective by giving partner the widest, cheapest window to take control and finish, while accepting that I cannot strongly contest the trick with my weak hand anyway.

partnerBelief.summary: Partner (player 2) holds only 2 cards, so they are likely preserving one or two strong cards (trumps or high non-trumps) to finish. Because no cards have been played, partner may be waiting for a low, cheap lead they can beat to seize control and play out. I should lead the card that is easiest for them to win without forcing them to spend a premium card unnecessarily.

opponentBelief.summary: Opponents (players 1 and 3, 3 cards each) want to prevent partner from going out. If I lead high, opponents can let it pass or win with moderate cards; if I lead low, they may win cheaply with any middling card and then attack partner. However, with only low cards myself, I cannot realistically control the trick, so the priority is feeding partner rather than trying to block opponents directly.

actionRationale.primaryReason: The partner-near-finish scenario dominates. Leading the lowest card (3♦) best supports the team objective by giving partner the widest, cheapest window to take control and finish, while accepting that I cannot strongly contest the trick with my weak hand anyway.
